$comment: MailAegis update & announcement channel. Edit this file, commit it, push it — every running copy of MailAegis picks it up within a few hours. See channel/README.md for the field reference.

latest.notes: Sign in to Microsoft 365 and Google without an app password.
changelog: OAuth 2.0 for IMAP and SMTP. Run "mailaegis oauth", sign in in your browser, and no password is involved — the only way in to a Microsoft tenant with basic authentication switched off, which Microsoft is doing progressively.
Non-English subjects are decoded correctly. Long headers are split by the sender's client, and MailAegis was leaving a space at the break, so every rule matching on the subject missed.
Sender allow/block lists — and an allow-list entry only counts when the message also proves it really came from that sender.
Audit trail of every decision, including the overrides, forwarded to your SIEM if you want it.
Undo send, the raw source viewer, defanged IOC export, signatures, drafts and auto-refresh.
Several mailboxes at once, compose with outbound scanning, search operators and dark mode.
Android app and an installable PWA, so a phone works too.

announcements.title: MailAegis is open source — and free forever.
announcements.body: If it saves your team from one wire-transfer fraud, a star on GitHub is all we ask.
